title: Submodular Reasoning Optimization: A Diminishing-Return Paradigm for Self-Regularized Large Reasoning Models
author: Zhang, Jincheng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) significantly improve performance by generating long-chain reasoning, but this also introduces serious problems of redundant reasoning and computational waste. Most existing methods rely on external signals or post-processing mechanisms, lacking a theoretical foundation for structural constraints from within the reasoning generation mechanism itself. This paper proposes a novel training and tuning paradigm—Submodular Reasoning Optimization (SRO)—which directly embeds the principle of diminishing marginal returns from submodular function optimization into the reasoning trajectory generation process. It treats reasoning as a set-building problem and imposes global structural constraints on the reasoning steps through the submodular objective function. This method no longer relies solely on token-level probabilities but introduces a set-level value function, enabling the model to automatically suppress redundant reasoning and prioritize reasoning steps with the highest information gain during generation. Theoretically, SRO transforms the reasoning generation problem into a greedy optimization process with near-optimal guarantees, achieving a balance between reasoning efficiency and correctness. This paradigm fundamentally reconstructs the training objective of LRMs, providing a new direction for building efficient and self-regulating reasoning systems.
steps_raw: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) significantly improve performance by generating long-chain reasoning, but this also introduces serious problems of redundant reasoning and computational waste. Most existing methods rely on external signals or post-processing mechanisms, lacking a theoretical foundation for structural constraints from within the reasoning generation mechanism itself. This paper proposes a novel training and tuning paradigm—Submodular Reasoning Optimization (SRO)—which directly embeds the principle of diminishing marginal returns from submodular function optimization into the reasoning trajectory generation process. It treats reasoning as a set-building problem and imposes global structural constraints on the reasoning steps through the submodular objective function. This method no longer relies solely on token-level probabilities but introduces a set-level value function, enabling the model to automatically suppress redundant reasoning and prioritize reasoning steps with the highest information gain during generation. Theoretically, SRO transforms the reasoning generation problem into a greedy optimization process with near-optimal guarantees, achieving a balance between reasoning efficiency and correctness. This paradigm fundamentally reconstructs the training objective of LRMs, providing a new direction for building efficient and self-regulating reasoning systems.
